events.text: Patricia White, 62-year-old Hispanic female, Current smoker (1 ppd). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Adenocarcinoma of the Right upper lobe of lung. Clinical staging: T2aN2M0, AJCC Stage IIIA. ICD-10: C34.11. Stage III non-small cell lung cancer with EGFR L858R mutation.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 elevated transaminases. ALT 95 U/L, AST 78 U/L. Treatment held one week, then resumed at reduced dose.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for restaging after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Underwent surgical resection: Right upper lobectomy with mediastinal lymph node dissection. Estimated blood loss 300 mL. Length of stay 3 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated adjuvant targeted therapy: Osimertinib 80 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on adjuvant targeted therapy.
Clinical visit for follow-up on adjuvant targeted therapy.
PET/CT performed for restaging on adjuvant targeted therapy.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.

documents.text:
BRAIN MRI WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Patricia White
MRN: patient_e28bd3f7bece
Date of Study: 2022-01-17

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:
BRAIN: No intracranial mass, hemorrhage, or acute infarct. Ventricles normal in size.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, as described.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Patricia White
MRN: patient_e28bd3f7bece
Specimen ID: S2022-99095
Date of Procedure: 2022-01-22
Date of Report: 2022-01-24

SPECIMEN: Right upper lobe of lung, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Adenocarcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 7.6 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
TTF-1: Positive
Napsin-A: Positive
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
PD-L1 (22C3): TPS 40%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Patricia White - Right upper lobe of lung" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 7.6 x 5.3 x 3.8 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Thoracic Surgery

Patient: Patricia White
MRN: patient_e28bd3f7bece
Date: 2022-01-28
Provider: Dr. Amanda Foster

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. White is a 62-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung, IIIA. Patient was referred by primary care physician after abnormal imaging. Stage III non-small cell lung cancer with EGFR L858R mutation. Pathology confirmed adenocarcinoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports incidental finding on routine screening.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- hypertension
- coronary artery disease

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- No prior surgeries

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 10 years ago)
- Alcohol: Social drinker (2-3 drinks/week)
- Occupation: Nurse (retired)
- Lives with alone

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with colon cancer at age 70.

ALLERGIES: Codeine (nausea)

MEDICATIONS:
- Lisinopril 10 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: intermittent headaches, fatigue, joint pain. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 122/78, HR 68, RR 19, Temp 97.7F, SpO2 96% on RA, Wt 69.4 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 3.4, Hgb 11.7, Plt 237, Cr 0.98

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
62-year-old female with IIIA adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. initial oncology consultation.

1. Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung, Stage IIIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend neoadjuvant chemotherapy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Port placement scheduled
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD
Thoracic Surgery

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Patricia White
MRN: patient_e28bd3f7bece
Date of Report: 2022-02-08
Specimen: Right upper lobe of lung (FNA cell block)
Diagnosis: Adenocarcinoma
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (523 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 6.2 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G) - Missense (Exon 21) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R248W - Missense (Exon 7)
  RB1 Loss - Deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.V2424G - VUS
  BRCA2 p.K1003E - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No ALK, ROS1, RET, or NTRK fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - EGFR L858R: FDA-approved targeted therapies include osimertinib (preferred), erlotinib, afatinib, gefitinib.
  - PD-L1 expression should be assessed for immunotherapy eligibility.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 450x coverage.
Tumor content: 71%.

Electronically signed by: Andrew Patel, MD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Patricia White
MRN: patient_e28bd3f7bece
Specimen ID: S2022-25583
Date of Procedure: 2022-06-11
Date of Report: 2022-06-16

SPECIMEN: Right upper lobe of lung, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Adenocarcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.2 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
TTF-1: Positive
Napsin-A: Positive
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
PD-L1 (22C3): TPS 40%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Patricia White - Right upper lobe of lung" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 5.2 x 3.6 x 2.6 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A8.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Patricia White
MRN: patient_e28bd3f7bece
Date of Study: 2022-10-08

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. restaging on adjuvant targeted therapy.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Irregular mass in the right upper lobe of lung measuring 3.0 x 2.4 cm. Decreased in size compared to prior.

MEDIASTINUM: Paratracheal lymphadenopathy, largest node measuring 1.9 cm in short axis.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, decreased.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Prior study dated approximately 9 weeks ago available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Irregular mass in the right upper lobe of lung measuring 2.3 x 1.8 cm. Decreased in size compared to prior.

MEDIASTINUM: Subcarinal lymphadenopathy, largest node measuring 2.4 cm in short axis.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.
